Master Logic Through Competition

Watch algorithms race on identical datasets in real-time. Tune every variable, predict winners, and develop algorithmic intuition no textbook can give you.

Where Do You Want To Go?

Pick your destination — race algorithms, learn the theory, or explore the code.

🏁

The Arena

Configure races, set custom datasets, and watch algorithms compete side-by-side with live analytics.

Enter Arena →
🧠

Learn

Deep-dive into every algorithm with complexity tables, pseudocode, full source code, and mini-simulations.

Start Learning →
🔍

Searching

Visualise Linear, Binary, Jump, and Interpolation search step-by-step on your own array.

Explore Search →
🕸️

Graphs

BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, A*, Floyd-Warshall — all visually traced on live node graphs.

Graph Theory →

How It Works

01

Pick Algorithms

Select 2–6 algorithms from the sorting, searching, or graph category.

02

Configure Data

Choose a preset dataset (random, reversed, nearly sorted) or type your own values.

03

Race!

Hit Start and watch algorithms compete. Pause, step through, or crank the speed.

04

Analyse

Review the leaderboard, ops count, time, and the live complexity graph to build intuition.

About AlgoRace

Built as the core instructional tool for CS402 Algorithms at RGPV B.Tech CSE Semester IV. AlgoRace bridges the gap between abstract Big-O notation and real-world performance through live, side-by-side competition.

Every algorithm is implemented as an ES6 Generator so it can be paused, resumed, and stepped through one operation at a time — giving you the granular insight you need.

HTML5 Canvas Web Audio API Chart.js Vite ES6 Generators
